_note: Channel manifest for recursive.eco. This makes the repo self-describing: recursive.eco can 'import this repo as a channel' by reading this file from raw.githubusercontent.com/<owner>/<repo>/<branch>/recursive-eco.json. It declares the channel's identity (rendered as a channel-document, document_data.is_channel=true), where its grammars live, and who maintains it. Membership lives in `tools` (channel_slug). See recursive-eco/docs/DESIGN-import-channel-from-github.md and recursive-eco/docs/DESIGN-channel-as-document.md.

channel.name: The Recursive Tarot
channel.description: The open, public-domain tarot library — decks, people, books, and courses — bound to its GitHub repo.

hero.title: The Recursive Tarot
hero.subtitle: An open, public-domain tarot library — held to the historical record, read as a gate, not a fate.
hero.body_md:
Every deck here is dated and attributed; the history is corrected, not mythologized. Built in the open and bound to its GitHub repo — fork it, correct it, or cast with it.

A card is a prompt, not a prophecy: read to know yourself, not to surrender yourself.

grammars._exclude_note: all-decks/tree/people/books are generated meta or reference apparatus (provenance:reference), not castable decks. Only public, complete deck grammars are imported as channel members; drafts/stubs are skipped on import.
